visual_inventory.subject: Razeen Shaheed — faux.thinker AI avatar
visual_inventory.framing: medium-tight, head + torso + occasional hand gestures enter frame from bottom
visual_inventory.backdrop: warm-LED plant shelf, trailing pothos, hexagonal wall panels, chair back bottom-left (identical to Video 2)
visual_inventory.lighting: warm 2800-3200K key + rim from shelf, matches Video 2 — same session or same setup
visual_inventory.gesture: HIGH gesture density — hands visible entering frame in ~40% of frames (f_0050, f_0100, f_0130, f_0180). Affects where we can safely place bottom-center captions

beats.vo: Codex just stopped being a coding tool.
OpenAI shipped six updates. One actually matters.
Codex now runs multiple agents on your machine. One fixes a bug, one runs the test suite, one reviews the output — while you sleep. And when you wake up, it tells you what to work on next.
The AI is no longer waiting for work. It is queuing yours.
beats.function: perspective reversal — great emphasis slam candidate

beats.vo: For a solo agency, this is not a productivity boost. It is the first version of having staff.
beats.function: the killer line — must-land emphasis slam

beats.vo: I am not switching from Claude, but I am running this on a side project for two weeks to see what breaks.
The question is not which AI you talk to. It is which one you let run your machine.
beats.function: reframe the whole AI category — emphasis slam candidate

beats.vo: Are you trying it? Drop the model you are sticking with.

known_whisper_mishears_corrected.note: This is the entire payline — must be right

what_maps_cleanly_to_v12: ProfileCard at open (no name spoken in script — would anchor to t=0.4s)
Light leaks + shutter SFX between beats
Single-word red captions at yPercent 78 (but subject to hand-gesture conflict)
what_is_NEW_and_unique_to_this_video: Multi-agent orchestration visual — no existing component; needs a new <MultiAgentOrchestra> that shows 3 parallel lanes with live status indicators (bug-fixer / test-suite / reviewer)
Terminal-simulation B-roll — fake a codex CLI showing 3 agents in parallel with colored prefixes + ticking output. This is the 'having staff' visual proof
Sleep-cycle metaphor — 'while you sleep' needs a visual — candidate: subtle moon/sun overlay, sleeping eye icon, or a ticking clock animation
Side-project-badge pill — 'SIDE PROJECT · 2 WEEKS' label for beat 6
ComparisonCard — two-column visual for 'talk to AI (left) vs let it run your machine (right)' reframe in beat 7. Much stronger than EmphasisCaption for comparison content
CommentPromptCard — replaces DMToast for beat 8. The CTA is an open question ('Drop the model you are sticking with'), not a DM trigger
'HAVING STAFF' is the signature line — deserves a typographic treatment stronger than standard EmphasisCaption. Consider QuoteCard with hand-drawn-underline on STAFF
faux_thinker_style_fit.hand_gesture_caption_conflict: Razeen's hands enter frame frequently between y=70% and y=95%. Default yPercent=78 for single-word captions will sometimes land on hands. Options: (a) move captions to yPercent=85 (tighter to bottom); (b) add a caption-bg translucent pill so gesture contrast is preserved; (c) use auto-shift — detect hand in frame and bump caption up

asset_gaps_before_render: - OpenAI Codex announcement page (openai.com/blog/ or openai.com/codex)
- Codex CLI / IDE integration screenshot (VSCode agent panel, or terminal with codex command)
rembg matting of avatar_insta3.mp4 (~1163 frames, ~13 min CPU)
New Remotion components to build (see above)
No SFX additions — reuse sfx_pack/ (braam, swoosh, click, shutter) + notification for CTA
